data.title: Trump says Qatar agrees to buy 160 Boeing jets
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) +1.7% in Wednesday's trading to a 52-week high of $209.65 after Qatar agrees to buy 160 of the company's jets, valued at more than $200B, the White House said in a post on X.

The agreement for the Qatar Airways purchase was signed during President Trump's visit to the country; Boeing (NYSE:BA) CEO Kelly Ortberg attended the ceremony with Trump and Qatar's Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al-Thani.

More to come...

data.title: PVH rises over 6% after upgrade at Jefferies
data.description:
Shares of PVH Corporation (NYSE:PVH) jumped over 6% after Jefferies upgraded its rating to Buy from Hold, banking on the company’s new management initiatives, including supply chain efficiencies and cost savings supported by buybacks.

The brokerage said the bullish rating is also driven by compelling risk/reward opportunity as it believes China regulation risk is meaningfully priced in at the current valuation.

“We see upside as strategic initiatives across product, pricing, inventory management, marketing/celebrity endorsements for brand heat w/ younger consumers, and refreshed brand leadership take greater hold,” noted Ashley Helgans, adding that, “Europe (~half of revs) order books inflecting positive is also important leading indicator, while improved macro could help.”

However, the brokerage firm warned that the main macro risk currently is PVH being on China's Unreliable Entity List, which could restrict PVH from operating in the region (~20% of EBIT).

“While it is hard to predict timing, a resolution or outsource to a license provider could serve as a significant catalyst.”

The brokerage firm increased its price target for PVH to $105 from $70.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating System, Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts are also bullish about the stock and rated it as Buy.

However, shares of PVH declined nearly 24% so far this year, underperforming the broader S&P 500 Index, which gained 0.08% during the same period.

data.title: Establishment Labs rises as Needham upgrades on easing trade war
data.description:
Shares of medical device company, Establishment Labs (NASDAQ:ESTA) rose more than 5% on Wednesday after investment bank Needham upgraded the stock to a BUY, citing easing of the trade war, which has reduced concerns regarding ESTA’s international business. 

Analysts were concerned that the trade war might cause the global economy to slow and hurt ESTA’s international sales.  However, with recent deals with the UK and China, fears regarding a potential trade war have significantly deescalated. 

Needham believes that Establishment Labs’ minimally invasive Mia and Preserve procedures can become significant growth drivers outside the U.S. 

“We believe that Preservé has a much larger market opportunity since it is intended for "day-to-day" breast augmentation patients,” analysts added. 

Needham noted in its note that the company’s management has been bullish on US Motiva launch, increasing analysts’ confidence that it can be a source of upside in 2025. 

Needham has a price target of $48 for ESTA. The stock has declined more than 21% so far this year.

data.title: Cboe stock dips after double-downgrade to Underweight at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE) shares slid 2% In Wednesday morning trading after Morgan Stanley double-downgraded the stock to Underweight from Overweight, citing tariff de-escalation with China that dampens volatility and may limit index option volume growth on the derivatives exchange.

"With the announcement of China tariffs being slashed to 30% (from 145%) over the weekend, which we note is lower than the 43% baseline that our strategists had expected at year-end, we adjust our outlook on CBOE," analyst Michael Cyprys wrote in a note to clients, noting "the substantial reduction in equity index volatility and reduction in tail risk."

In Q1 2025, the company's options revenue per contract retreated 4% from a year earlier, due to a mix shift, with index options representing a lower percentage of total options volume.

Morgan Stanley's Underweight rating diverges from the SA Quant system rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Hold.

data.title: Conduit appoints CEO
data.description:
Bermuda-based reinsurer Conduit Holdings (OTCPK:CNDHF) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Neil Eckert as CEO, effective immediately.

Eckert has been serving as the interim CEO since late March, following the retirement of Trevor Carvey.

Additionally, Senior Independent Director Rebecca Shelley has been appointed interim non-executive chair.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: SMCI, AMD, SONY, and more
data.description:
Stock futures held steady on Wednesday, following a mixed session, as investors assessed easing trade tensions and broader market signals.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) +7% — Shares rose after Raymond James initiated coverage with an “Outperform” rating and $41 PT, highlighting the AI server maker as a "near AI pure play." Analyst Simon Leopold noted AI platforms make up ~70% of SMCI’s revenue, with the company capturing 31% of the branded AI server market. Despite near-term risks tied to tariffs and tech transitions (e.g., NVIDIA's Hopper to Blackwell), long-term AI demand remains a strong growth driver. Leopold sees SMCI well-positioned between traditional IT giants and contract manufacturers, offering customized solutions with scaled manufacturing. Key catalysts include hyperscale AI growth, NVIDIA’s new platform ramp, and enterprise penetration in verticals like finance and healthcare.
  • Sony (NYSE:SONY) +6% — Shares rose after the company announced a ¥250B ($1.7B) share buyback and reported Q4 operating income of ¥203.6B, topping estimates of ¥192.2B despite an 11% Y/Y decline. Sony also unveiled plans for a partial spinoff of its financial unit, with 80% of shares to be distributed to shareholders via dividends; the unit will be classified as a discontinued operation from this quarter. However, the rally was tempered by a disappointing outlook for FY2026, as the company flagged a ¥100B ($700M) hit from U.S. tariffs, guiding to flat operating profit of ¥1.28T, below the ¥1.5T analyst consensus.
  • AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) +6% — Shares soared after the company announced a new $6B share buyback program, supplementing the $4B remaining under its previous authorization as of March 29. “Our expanded share repurchase program reflects the board’s confidence in AMD’s strategic direction, growth prospects, and ability to consistently generate strong free cash flow,” said Chair and CEO Dr. Lisa Su, emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and a commitment to shareholder returns.

Biggest stock losers 

  • Alcon (NYSE:ALC) -7% — Shares dropped after Q1 results and updated guidance fell short of expectations, with the company flagging a gross tariff impact of ~$80M, expected to pressure sales and costs. While Alcon aims to offset the tariff hit via operational and FX actions, its revised outlook reflects dilutive effects from recent deals, weighing on core operating margin by ~80 bps and EPS by ~$0.10. FY2025 net sales outlook was raised to $10.4–$10.5B (from $10.2–$10.4B), but core op. margin was trimmed to 20–21% (from 21–22%), and core EPS was cut to $3.05–$3.15 (vs. prior $3.15–$3.25 and consensus $3.24).
  • American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) -15% — Shares slumped after the company pulled its full-year forecast, citing macro uncertainty, and said it is “actively evaluating” its forward plans. For Q1, AEO expects revenue to decline 5% to ~$1.1B (vs. $1.09B consensus) and anticipates a GAAP operating loss of ~$85M and an adjusted loss of ~$68M. The results reflect higher-than-expected promotional activity, a $75M inventory write-down on spring and summer merchandise, and a $17M restructuring charge tied to the closure of two fulfillment centers. Comparable sales are projected to fall ~3%, with American Eagle down 2% and Aerie down 4%. CEO Jay Schottenstein acknowledged merchandising missteps, but noted inventory is now better aligned heading into Q2.
  • Aurora Innovation (NASDAQ:AUR) -13% — Shares plummeted after Uber (NYSE:UBER) announced a $1B private offering of Exchangeable Senior Notes due 2028, initially convertible into Aurora stock at an implied price of ~$8.50 per share. The structure sparked investor concerns about potential dilution, as each $1,000 note is exchangeable into 117.6471 shares. Uber, which acquired a 26% stake in Aurora in December 2020 as part of the sale of its autonomous driving unit, Advanced Technologies Group, and has since held board representation through CEO Dara Khosrowshahi, said it may use the proceeds—estimated at up to $1.13B—for general corporate purposes, including strategic investments.
  • Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) -4% — Shares fell after Jefferies downgraded the stock to Hold from Buy, pointing to weakening demand and a lack of near-term catalysts, such as progress updates on the R2 model. While the firm acknowledged Rivian’s brand strength and software capabilities, it flagged the need for the EV maker to demonstrate sustainable gross margin and greater capital efficiency. Jefferies cut its 2025 revenue forecast on lower unit sales expectations but expects Rivian to remain funded through the R2 launch in H1 2026, with $1.5B in net liquidity by year-end. The firm set a $16 price target.

data.title: Informatica unveils agentic AI offerings; collaborations with Microsoft, others
data.description:
Informatica made a flurry of announcements at its Informatica World event in Las Vegas about its suite of AI-powered products and several collaborations, including one with Microsoft (MSFT).

Informatica AI agents:

Informatica unveiled agentic AI offerings on Its AI-powered cloud data management platform.

The company unveiled AI Agent Engineering — a new service that enables enterprises to build, connect and manage intelligent AI agent workflows. AI Agent Engineering is expected to be available globally in fall of 2025 as part of Informatica’s cloud data management platform.

Informatica also launched Claire Agents, a suite of autonomous digital assistants designed to augment enterprise data management. The Claire Agents are expected to be available in preview starting in the fall of 2025, according to the company.

Informatica said its strategic approach to Agentic AI expands on its AI innovation history, which includes the launch of  Claire GPT,  Claire  Copilot and GenAI blueprints for major cloud ecosystem partners.

The company added that Claire Copilot is generally available for data integration and cloud application integration, starting May.

"With the launch of CLAIRE® Agents and AI Agent Engineering, we are redefining what is possible in data management and AI orchestration," said Informatica CEO Amit Walia.

Informatica and Oracle platform:

Separately, Informatica noted the upcoming availability of its Master Data Management, or MDM, service and Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform service on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, or OCI (ORCL), enabling customers to use Informatica MDM natively in their OCI environment.

The company said it has been designated as a preferred partner for enterprise cloud data integration, data governance and MDM for data warehouse and lakehouse solutions on OCI. 

MDM service helps unify data across an enterprise. The availability of Informatica MDM software as a service, or SaaS, on OCI enables companies to create a single and unified view of their business data, according to the company.

Informatica and Microsoft:

Informatica announced a strategic agreement with Microsoft under which the companies will deliver unified, AI-powered solutions that combine Informatica’s Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform with key Microsoft services, including Microsoft Fabric and Microsoft Azure.

Informatica’s cloud data management platform is available as a SaaS offering on Azure and through Azure Marketplace.

"By integrating our cloud data management capabilities directly into Microsoft Fabric, and building CLAIRE Copilots leveraging Azure OpenAI Service, we're empowering business users to build enterprise grade analytics and AI applications,” said Krish Vitaldevara, Chief Product Officer at Informatica.

Informatica and Amazon Bedrock:

Informatica announced "new recipes" to develop AI Agents, built with Amazon Bedrock — an Amazon service that offers foundation models from Amazon and other AI companies, along with tools for developers to create AI applications securely and privately.

Informatica said the service enables joint customers to build, connect, and manage AI agent workflows with trusted data and no-code simplicity.

The company announced two new recipes for AI agents built with Amazon Bedrock: Supply Chain Management and Simple REACT Agent AI are now generally available.

In addition, the company announced the achievement of Amazon Generative AI Competency and innovations across its analytics portfolio for AWS services, including Amazon Redshift and Amazon SageMaker Lakehouse.

Informatica and Salesforce’s Agentforce:

Informatica said it plans to expand its partnership with Salesforce (CRM) to integrate its Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform with Salesforce’s Agentforce — a service which helps users to build, customize, and deploy autonomous AI Agents.

The company unveiled Agentforce for Sales and Agentforce for Service. Informatica MDM SaaS will use records— data collected from several enterprise systems — to enrich AI agents built with Agentforce, supporting critical sales and service use cases across business to customers and business to business environment.

Informatica and Databricks:

Informatica announced an expanded partnership with Databricks under which customers could modernize their on-premises data lakes by combining Informatica’s Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform with Databricks' Data Intelligence Platform to provide a complete data foundation for their future analytics and AI workloads.

data.title: Deere Q2 2025 Earnings Preview: Margin debate takes center stage
data.description:
Deere (NYSE:DE) is scheduled to announce Q2 earnings results on Thursday, May 15th, before market open.

Analysts expect the firm to earn $5.62 on revenue of $10.79B (-29.2% Y/Y).

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 3 upward revisions and 11 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 7 downward.

The negative skew in estimate revisions reflects tempered expectations for the quarter, likely due to softening demand across the agriculture and construction sectors and ongoing destocking by dealers following post-pandemic supply normalization.

Investors will be watching closely for management commentary on order trends and dealer inventory levels, especially in large ag equipment, and any updates to full-year guidance.

According to Oppenheimer, the bigger debate among investors is the margin outlook, which remains surprisingly resilient despite steep production cuts. The firm notes that Deere is still guiding toward a healthy 30% consolidated incremental margin from Q1 to Q2, citing two key drivers: delayed delivery of margin-rich seasonal products, expected to land in Q2 and improving margins in the construction & forestry division.

However, looking beyond Q2, Oppenheimer has moderated its full-year margin outlook due to rising price/cost pressures from tariffs.
